Just speak and let the language flow. If

talk about today's topic. It's one of my
favorite topics. It's called the power
of small habits. Many people believe
success comes from big actions. They
think they must study for hours every
day. They think they must change
everything at once. But real change
doesn't work like that. Real change
doesn't happen in one big moment. It
happens in many small moments repeated
every day. Think about brushing your
teeth. You don't brush for two hours
once a month. You brush for 2 minutes
every day. That small habit keeps your
teeth healthy. Learning English works
the same way. It's not about one big
weekend of study. It's about small daily
actions. 5 minutes of listening, 5
minutes of speaking, 5 minutes of
shadowing. These moments may feel small
right now, but over time they create big
results. And there is science behind
this. When you repeat a small action
every day, your brain changes. Your
brain builds a new path, a new road. At
first, that road is weak and slow. But
every time you practice, it becomes
stronger. It becomes faster. At the
beginning, speaking English feels
difficult. You stop, you think, you
search for words. That's normal. That's
part of learning. But with small daily
practice, something beautiful happens.
Your words come faster. Your tongue
moves more easily and speaking becomes
more automatic. Automatic means your
mouth speaks without your brain
panicking. Small habits are powerful
because they are easy. They don't need
strong motivation. You don't need to
feel excited. You just do them. And
that's when real learning begins. Here's
a very important secret. If you want to
build a habit, start small. Very small.
If you plan to study 1 hour every day,
you may quit after one week. But if you
plan just 5 minutes, you'll probably do
it. It's very hard to fail 5 minutes,
right? You can shadow English while
making coffee. You can repeat sentences
while brushing your hair. You can
quietly practice while walking. Small
moments are easy to repeat and
repeatable moments grow. 5 minutes
becomes 10. 10 minutes becomes a habit.
And this is how real fluency begins. And
when you keep doing this, 5 minutes
slowly becomes 10. 10 becomes 20. And
very soon English becomes a natural part
of your life.
Let me share a short real story with
you. I once worked with a learner who
was extremely shy. Even very simple
English sentences scared him. He knew
words. He understood English. But when
it was time to speak, his mind froze.
So, we didn't chase big goals. We didn't
push long study hours. We started with
one tiny habit. Every morning, he spoke
to himself in English for just 2
minutes. At the beginning, his sentences
were very simple. I'm getting ready. I'm
making coffee. I'm going to work. That's
all. But something interesting happened.
After a few weeks, his sentences became
longer. After 2 months, he could tell
short stories about his day. And after 6
months, he joined an English-speaking
club. 2 minutes changed everything. Not
because 2 minutes is magical, but
because 2 minutes was repeatable. It
transformed his confidence and
confidence always comes before fluency.
So here is my message for you today.
Don't chase big goals. Build small
